data.title: Meta Platforms Inc. cuts jobs, leading shares to rise; CEO Zuckerberg's wealth also increases. Stock closed at $661.53, opened at $664.50, hit high of $676.10, closed at $673.07, up 1.7%. Layoffs possible in January with 30% budget cuts for metaverse unit, which lost over $70 billion since 2021.
data.description: On Thursday, Meta Platforms Inc's (NASDAQ: META) shares rose following an announcement of job cuts within the company. The surge in stock prices saw Meta CEO and co-founder Mark Zuckerberg's wealth increase. The stock opened higher on Thursday at $664.50, moving from a previous close of $661.53 to reach an intraday high of $676.10, ultimately closing at $673.07, marking a 1.7% increase. The positive trend continued into Friday, possibly influenced by the news. Reports suggest that Meta may initiate layoffs as early as January, with a projected 30% reduction in the budget for the metaverse group. Notably, the metaverse unit has incurred losses exceeding $70 billion since 2021. This shift in focus signifies a significant transformation for the company.

data.title: Halper Sadeh LLC urges DocGo Inc. shareholders to act promptly regarding potential rights breaches. NY, Dec. 5, 2025.
data.description: Investors are urged to promptly reach out to the company as there may be a restricted window to protect their interests. It is crucial for shareholders to take action swiftly to safeguard their rights.

data.title: OCP unveils 10-ft RoboCop statue in Detroit, marking 20 years of defictionalization project.
data.description: In Detroit on December 5, 2025, Omni Consumer Products (OCP) celebrated the accomplishment of a significant project lasting almost 15 years. OCP, known for its connection to the fictional group in the film RoboCop, has successfully defictionalized the cyborg hero through this initiative.

data.title: Corporate pension funding levels increased in November for 8th month: PFI funded ratio rose to 107.1%, discount rates up to 5.34%.
data.description: In Seattle, Milliman, Inc., a leading global consulting and actuarial firm, unveiled its latest update on the Milliman 100 Pension Funding Index (PFI). This index delves into the financial status of the top 100 U.S. corporate pension plans. In November, the PFI's funded ratio saw a slight increase from 107.0% in October to 107.1% by November 30. The uptick was influenced by a 1-basis-point climb in discount rates, rising to 5.34% in November. This adjustment led to a $3 billion reduction in plan liabilities, counterbalancing market returns of only 0.44%.

data.title: Bitcoin traders remain cautious as risk-off signals trigger despite optimism about a sub-$100K BTC being a bargain. Is $100K feasible by 2025’s end?
data.description: As Bitcoin's price rebound waned nearing the weekly close, traders are cautioning a significant correction risk for BTC, signaled by various risk-off metrics. The possibility of reaching $100,000 by the conclusion of 2025 is being deliberated.

data.title: PotlatchDeltic declares $0.45/share distribution to stockholders of record by Dec 15, payable Dec 31. REIT with 2.1M acres of timberlands.
data.description: In Spokane, Washington, PotlatchDeltic Corporation (Nasdaq: PCH) announced a quarterly distribution of $0.45 per share for its common stock. The payment is scheduled for December 31, 2025, and will be given to stockholders of record on December 15, 2025. PotlatchDeltic, a prominent Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT), possesses 2.1 million acres of timberlands across states like Alabama, Arkansas, Georgia, Idaho, Louisiana, Mississippi, and South.

data.title: Asetek A/S discloses trade transactions by managers per Market Abuse Regulation art. 19 on December 5, 2025 in Denmark.
data.description: In Aalborg, Denmark, on December 5, 2025, Asetek A/S disclosed under the Market Abuse Regulation art. 19 that notifications were received concerning transactions in the company's shares and related securities by individuals discharging managerial responsibilities.
